title.zh: 司掌和山、能动天地之气的吉神
title.en: Auspicious God of Mount He Who Stirs the Breath of Heaven and Earth

summary.zh: 和山之神，人形虎尾，出入有光，能搅动天地之气；郭璞注引夏后孔甲田猎遇大风晦冥事为其注脚。
summary.en: The auspicious god of Mount He, human in form with a tiger's tail, moving in light and able to stir the qi of heaven and earth — blamed by commentators for the storm that once bewildered King Kongjia of Xia.

description.zh:
《中次三经》末山和山“五曲，九水出焉”，九条水合流北注黄河，水中多苍玉，“实惟河之九都”。吉神泰逢司掌此山：“其状如人而虎尾，是好居于萯山之阳，出入有光。泰逢神动天地气也。”人形拖一条虎尾，爱住在萯山向阳的南坡，出入时带着光，能搅动天地之气。

“动天地气”四字有一桩旧事作注脚。郭璞注引《吕氏春秋》：夏后孔甲在萯山田猎，天起大风，白昼晦暗如夜，孔甲迷了路，走进一户民家。注家以为那场大风正是泰逢所为——号称吉神，翻起脸来也能搅得天昏地暗。

中次三经末尾开列祠礼：泰逢与熏池、武罗三神同礼，“皆一牡羊副，婴用吉玉”——劈开一头公羊为牲，以吉玉礼神。三神并列，同领敖岸、青要、和山这一列黄河南岸的山。
description.en:
Mount He, last peak of the third central sequence, bends five times, and nine streams rise on it, joining to flow north into the Yellow River; the text calls it 'the ninefold gathering-place of the River.' Its warden is the auspicious god Taifeng, human in form with a tiger's tail, who favors the sunny south side of Mount Fu and moves in an aura of light. The classic closes the notice with a single line of power: Taifeng stirs the qi of heaven and earth.

Guo Pu's commentary glosses that line with a story from the Lüshi Chunqiu. King Kongjia of the Xia was hunting on Mount Fu when a great wind rose and the day turned dark as night; the bewildered king blundered into a commoner's house. That storm, the commentators held, was Taifeng's doing — an auspicious god who could still black out the sky.

The sequence ends with his cult: Taifeng, Xunchi and Wuluo are served alike, a ram cleft in sacrifice and fine jade presented to the gods, three wardens sharing the hills along the south bank of the Yellow River.

value.en: Mount He, ninefold gathering-place of the River
Human form with a tiger's tail
A ram cleft in sacrifice, fine jade to greet the god

sources.en: Guo Pu, Commentary on the Shanhaijing
